rationale: Building V2 later; don't double-scope now
Your actual lived experience; business lane becomes v2
Preserves future paid tier option without publishing pressure
Filters for real intent, no subscription burden
Asset beats content treadmill for solo builders
Demonstration of belief, not just information

decision: Claude Code owns the repo, Strategy Claude owns content
rationale: Prevents the two Claudes from stepping on each other's work

decision: v1.0 ships landing and admin only
rationale: Collect emails and validate demand before building the product delivery flow

decision: V1.0 ships with admin-facing checklist only
rationale: User-facing checklists are v1.1 and v1.2, not v1.0, to preserve the 2-week ship date

decision: v1.1 is the User Setup Checklist (5-phase flow)
rationale: Real product delivery, requires user accounts and auth, scoped as its own major release

decision: v1.2 is the Dynamic Checklist generated from personal Nexus
rationale: Depends on v1.1 existing first; speced now but built later when we have real buyer feedback

decision: Landing page previews v1.1 and v1.2 features without promising delivery dates
rationale: Captures full vision for buyers while protecting launch timeline
